section: AP 17: Neuropathology Topics for Anatomic Pathology Residents
allocation_strategy: Clinical judgment prioritizing major brain tumors, vascular disorders, and essential diagnostic recognition

questions.topic: Basic neuroanatomy: cortex, white matter, deep nuclei
questions.education_objective: Identify basic neuroanatomic structures and understand their pathologic significance

questions.topic: CNS cell types: neurons, astrocytes, oligodendroglia
questions.education_objective: Distinguish major CNS cell types and recognize their morphologic features
Recognize reactive astrocytic changes and distinguish from neoplastic proliferation
Recognize glioblastoma morphologic features and apply WHO classification criteria
Distinguish IDH-mutant astrocytomas from glioblastoma and understand molecular classification
Classify meningioma variants and apply WHO grading criteria

questions.topic: Metastatic tumors to brain: patterns and primaries
questions.education_objective: Recognize metastatic tumor patterns and identify likely primary sites
Distinguish pilocytic astrocytoma from diffuse astrocytic tumors in pediatric cases
Differentiate schwannoma from neurofibroma using morphologic criteria

questions.topic: Cerebral infarction: acute vs. chronic changes
questions.education_objective: Recognize temporal evolution of cerebral infarction and understand vascular territories
Recognize subarachnoid hemorrhage patterns and identify berry aneurysms
Identify typical locations of hypertensive brain hemorrhage and understand mechanisms
Recognize HSV encephalitis morphology and understand temporal lobe predilection
Distinguish bacterial from viral meningitis using morphologic criteria

questions.topic: Traumatic brain injury: coup vs. contrecoup
questions.education_objective: Recognize traumatic brain injury patterns and understand biomechanical principles
